metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Tarkir: Dragonstorm is a dragon-focused Limited format where five-color "good stuff" decks have become dominant. The developers intentionally extended game length through toughness-focused sizing and efficient removal to make dragons viable at all rarities. The format rewards taking the best card each pack rather than forcing archetypes early.

color_notes.{G}{U}{R} Temur: Best clan. Strong fixing, excellent multicolor payoffs, and premium uncommons. Harmonize is the most consistent value mechanic.
color_notes.{U}{R}{W} Jeskai: Flurry delivers reliably and the clan supports multiple viable flavors (aggro, midrange, control good-stuff).
color_notes.{R}{W}{B} Mardu: Challenging but rewarding. Requires careful deckbuilding; Mobilize is the archetype glue.
color_notes.{B}{G}{U} Sultai: Solid graveyard synergies and fixing. Renew creatures range from mediocre to good.
color_notes.{W}{B}{G} Abzan: Endure can underperform without sufficient counter density.

key_gameplay_notes: Five-color "good stuff" piles built around Dragonstorm Globe are the dominant strategy.
Behold commons (Molten Exhale, Caustic Exhale, Dispelling Exhale) are premium removal and extend games.
Flurry is easier to trigger than initial predictions suggested — Jeskai card advantage payoffs scale fast.
Hybrid mana cards (Rakshasa's Bargain, Temur Tawnyback, Kin-Tree Severance) cost 3 or 4 mana depending on the draw — flexibility is valuable.
Two-color aggro decks punish greedy 4-5c piles with tight mana curves and premium removal.
Clan-specific payoffs often go late if the archetype isn't open.

mechanics.name: Endure (Abzan)
mechanics.summary: Value mechanic on Abzan creatures, not a required build-around. Overlaps with token payoffs and counter synergies.
mechanics.performance: Works best with counter density; otherwise underperforms.

mechanics.name: Flurry (Jeskai)
mechanics.summary: Triggered by casting two or more spells in a turn. Easier to achieve than expected; focus on card advantage to enable repeated triggers.
mechanics.performance: Reliable. Defines Jeskai tempo and control variants alike.

mechanics.name: Renew (Sultai)
mechanics.summary: Activated ability from the graveyard. Free-value Renew is the best version; creature Renew ranges from mediocre to good depending on rarity and color.
mechanics.performance: Light graveyard commitments slot into full Sultai strategies.

mechanics.name: Mobilize (Mardu)
mechanics.summary: Forms its own archetype. Original payoff predictions (Worthy Cost, Barrensteppe Siege) proved incorrect; the actual mobilize payoffs are different.
mechanics.performance: Hybrid mana flexibility makes mobilize-specific payoffs less critical than expected.

mechanics.name: Harmonize (Temur)
mechanics.summary: Primary value mechanic for Temur. Best with board-affecting spells.

mechanics.name: Omens
mechanics.summary: Enable dragon density across the set. Many rare and uncommon omens rank among the set's best cards.
mechanics.performance: High impact at all rarities; common omens are still solid.

mechanics.name: Behold
mechanics.summary: Efficient common cycle (Molten Exhale, Caustic Exhale, Dispelling Exhale, Piercing Exhale, Sagu Wildling) that extends game length and powers the slower formats.

mechanics.name: Hybrid Mana
mechanics.summary: Several spells with hybrid mana costs (Rakshasa's Bargain, Temur Tawnyback, Kin-Tree Severance) often cost 3 or 4 mana depending on draw, giving flexible color requirements.

mechanics.name: Surveil
mechanics.summary: Smoothing mechanic best in Sultai. Mostly enabled via Piercing Exhale and Boulderborn Dragon.
mechanics.performance: Light support; works as deck consistency rather than a payoff theme.

tier_1.name: 5c Dragonstorm (Globe Deck)
tier_1.strategy: Take the best card each pack: bombs first, then efficient removal, card advantage, fixing, and finally curve filler. Dragonstorm Globe is the centerpiece. Mardu being weaker creates space for greedy piles.

tier_1.name: Temur Stompy
tier_1.strategy: Play large creatures and removal. Temur Tawnyback is that good in multiple copies. Harmonize spells like Glacial Dragonhunt and Mammoth Bellow snowball.

tier_2.name: Jeskai Tempo
tier_2.strategy: Multiple flavors: (1) aggro/midrange using 2-drops like Jeskai Devotee with removal, (2) hyper-aggro with cheap creatures and flurry synergies, (3) control/good-stuff foundation for 4-5c piles.

tier_2.name: Sultai Midrange
tier_2.strategy: Light graveyard commitments to full strategies. Payoffs ranked: Lotuslight Dancers > Lasyd Prowler > Kishla Skimmer > Kheru Goldkeeper > Essence Anchor > Attuned Hunter.

tier_2.name: Mardu Aggro
tier_2.strategy: Base RW: fast pressure with a turn-5 clock focus. Base BW: build to critical mass with mobilize synergies. Stay 2-3 colors for curve consistency; avoid overextending mana bases.

tier_3.name: Abzan Midrange
tier_3.strategy: Flavor 1: standard midrange with token synergies. Flavor 2: counter-focused with aggressive payoffs like Synchronized Charge and Champion of Dusan.

tier_3.name: 2-Color Aggro
tier_3.strategy: Punish greedy 4-5c piles with tight mana curves and premium removal. Premium 2-drops and efficient removal are the engine.

top_busted_rares.name: Ugin, Eye of the Storms
top_busted_rares.note: Most broken; goes in every deck.

general_tips: Clan-specific payoffs often go late if the archetype isn't open — read signals before committing.
Behold commons are premium removal; draft them highly across colors.
Flurry is easier to trigger than initial predictions suggested — Jeskai card advantage scales.
Don't overvalue mobilize-specific payoffs; hybrid mana gives flexibility.
Two-color aggro decks punish greedy piles effectively — keep an eye on the metagame at your table.
Endure synergies work best with counter density.
Post-sideboard adjustments using cards like Riverwalk Technique help counter specific bombs.
